rationale: The current winner looks capacity-limited rather than step-limited: widening from baseline helped even with fewer tokens, and the run already fits comfortably under the 16MB artifact cap. This change keeps roughly the same total parameter and compute budget by trading one layer for more width (8x576 instead of 9x544), which should improve per-step quality on a 600s budget while reducing sequential depth a bit. I also tighten grouped int8 quantization from 64 to 32 columns, spending some of the remaining artifact headroom directly on lower roundtrip error for the final scored metric.
title: 8x576 same-budget model with finer grouped int8
